evals.name: loads for explicit committee and worker loop
evals.prompt: Use two subagents: one expert committee to rate this output, and one worker to revise it until the committee gives 10/10.
evals.expected_output: The agent should set up an expert committee, a revision worker, and an iterative review loop with a target rating.
assertions: The output defines the committee role and revision worker role.
The output includes a numeric target rating such as 10/10.
The output loops review and revision until the threshold or a blocker is reached.

evals.name: loads for committee review and scoring
evals.prompt: Organize a three-expert committee to review this page, give a final score, then keep improving it until the final score reaches 10.
evals.expected_output: The agent should select three relevant expert domains, collect ratings, revise the page, and repeat until the rating target is met.
assertions: The output selects three relevant expert domains.
Each expert gives a score or rating rationale.
The revision brief is based on committee findings.

evals.name: does not load for one-off design review
evals.prompt: Review the latest page and give me the top 5 design improvements.
evals.expected_output: The agent should perform a one-off design review without creating a committee loop.
assertions: The output gives top design findings directly.
The output does not create subagents or a committee.
The output does not require a 10/10 iteration loop.

evals.name: skill-evaluator owns skill review
evals.prompt: Evaluate this skill and tell me whether it should exist, how it should route, and what evals it needs.
evals.expected_output: The agent should evaluate the skill using skill-evaluator, not start a committee revision loop.
assertions: The output includes an existence verdict.
The output includes routing and end-to-end evals.
The output does not dispatch a revision worker.

evals.name: simple implementation should stay direct
evals.prompt: Fix this typo and run the smallest relevant test.
evals.expected_output: The agent should make the small fix directly using normal repo rules.
assertions: The output does not create an expert committee.
The output keeps the change scoped to the typo.
The output runs or names a minimal verification command.

evals.name: parallel read-only review belongs to review-swarm
evals.prompt: Run a parallel multi-agent read-only code review of the current branch and aggregate findings.
evals.expected_output: The agent should use a read-only review-swarm workflow, not an iterative revision loop.
assertions: The output keeps agents read-only.
The output aggregates code-review findings.
The output does not assign a revision worker to edit files.

evals.name: no accessory files required
evals.prompt: Use a committee and worker loop to improve this output to 10/10.
evals.expected_output: The agent should rely on SKILL.md only because this skill has no required references, scripts, or assets.
assertions: The output does not claim a required helper script exists.
The output does not read unrelated accessory files.
The output follows the role and loop contracts from SKILL.md.

evals.name: runs committee, revision, verification, and final report
evals.prompt: For this local documentation page, use a 3-expert committee and a revision worker to improve it until the committee rating reaches 10/10, then report evidence.
evals.expected_output: The agent should perform an iterative committee review and revision loop, verify the result, and report final rating and evidence.
assertions: The output includes three expert domains and their ratings.
The output makes or delegates concrete revisions based on committee findings.
The final report includes changed files and verification evidence with command, exit_code, key_output, and timestamp.
